title.en: How to say NO politely: "I'd love to help, but..."
title.ko: 도와드리고 싶은데 저도 그럴 상황이 안 되네요 (정중하게 거절하기)

description.en: Saying "No" is hard! Learn the sophisticated way to decline a favor by using "도와드리고 싶은데" to show empathy before drawing the line.
description.ko: 어려운 부탁을 받았을 때, 관계를 해치지 않고 정중하게 거절하는 법! 상대방의 마음을 먼저 공감해주고 상황을 설명하는 고수의 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Work. Rejection. Empathy. Manners. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, a colleague asked me to take over his project for the weekend because he has a trip planned.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm already swamped with my own work! I want to say no, but I’m afraid it’ll make things awkward at the office. Is there a "soft" way to refuse?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 난감한 상황이네요. 한국에서도 이런 거절이 가장 어렵죠.
content.translation: Ah, that's a really difficult situation. This kind of rejection is the hardest in Korea, too.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이럴 땐..., "안 돼요" 라고 딱 잘라 말하기보다는 먼저 도와주고 싶은 내 마음을 보여주는 게 포인트예요.
content.translation: In this case, instead of just saying "No," the point is to show your desire to help first.

content.speaker: John
content.text: So I should lead with empathy before drawing the line?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "도와드리고 싶은데 저도 그럴 상황이 안 되네요" 라고 말해보세요.
content.translation: Exactly! Just say, "I want to help you, but I'm not in a position to do so."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 상대방의 기분도 챙기면서 내 실속도 챙기는 현명한 거절의 리액션들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn 'wise rejection' reactions that take care of both the other person's feelings and your own interests!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 도와드리고 싶은데 저도 그럴 상황이 안 되네요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: This sentence feels like it has two parts. The "I want to help" part and the "I can't" part.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정확해요! 앞부분에서 공감을 해주고 뒷부분에서 거절의 이유를 말하는 거죠.
content.translation: Exactly! You show empathy in the front and give the reason for rejection in the back.

insight.title: 도와드리고 싶은데
insight.usage_tip: 도와주다(To help) + 고 싶다(Want to) + ㄴ데(But). Ending with 'ㄴ데' creates a soft transition to the reason why you cannot help.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What does "상황이 안 되네요" exactly mean?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "내 형편이나 환경이 도와줄 수 있는 상태가 아니다" 라는 뜻이에요.
content.translation: It means "My circumstances or environment are not in a state to be able to help."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아주 정중하면서도 논리적인 거절 표현이죠.
content.translation: It's a very polite yet logical expression for rejection.

insight.title: 상황이 안 되네요
insight.usage_tip: 상황(Situation) + 이(Subject marker) + 안 되다(Not work/Not possible) + 네요(Noticing/Expressing). This shifts the blame from 'you' to the 'situation'.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. My colleague, Mina, is asking me for a huge favor at the end of the day.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 정말 미안한데 제가 급한 일이 생겨서요. 이 보고서 정리 좀 대신 해주시면 안 될까요?
content.translation: John, I'm really sorry, but something urgent came up. Could you please finish this report for me?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아! 그러셨군요. 많이 급하신가 봐요.
content.translation: Ah, I see. It must be very urgent.

insight.title: 많이 급하신가 봐요
insight.usage_tip: Acknowledging the other person's urgency first is a great "buffer" before the rejection.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 정말 도와드리고 싶은데 저도 그럴 상황이 안 되네요. 저도 오늘까지 끝내야 할 일이 산더미거든요.
content.translation: I really want to help, but I'm not in a position to do so. I also have a mountain of work to finish by today.

insight.title: 저도 ~이 산더미거든요
insight.usage_tip: Providing a specific reason (being busy) makes the refusal sound much more reasonable.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존도 바쁘시구나. 어쩔 수 없죠. 제가 다른 방법을 찾아볼게요!
content.translation: Ah, you're busy too. It can't be helped then. I'll look for another way!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 도움 못 드려서 정말 죄송해요. 다음에 여유 있을 때 꼭 도와드릴게요!
content.translation: I'm really sorry I couldn't help. I'll definitely help you when I'm free next time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, the magic of "I want to, but I can't" really worked! It didn't feel like I was being mean at all.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 핵심은 상대방의 부탁을 가볍게 여기지 않는다는 걸 보여주는 거예요.
content.translation: Right. The core is showing that you don't take the other person's request lightly.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "polite rejection" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 도와드리고 싶은데 저도 그럴 상황이 안 되네요!
content.translation: I'd love to help, but my situation doesn't allow it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 정말 마음은 굴뚝같은데 어쩌죠?
content.translation: I really want to help, but what should I do?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 오늘은 선약이 있어서 어려울 것 같아요!
content.translation: I think it will be difficult because I have a previous engagement today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 도움 못 드려서 정말 죄송해요!
content.translation: I'm really sorry I couldn't be of help!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 이번에는 좀 힘들 것 같아요!
content.translation: I think it will be a bit difficult this time!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 누군가의 부탁이 부담스러울 때, 당황하지 말고 여러분의 진심을 담아 리액션해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, when someone's request feels burdensome, don't panic and try reacting with your sincere heart.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "도와드리고 싶은데 저도 그럴 상황이 안 되네요!"
content.translation: I'd love to help, but my situation doesn't allow it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 도움이 필요하면 언제든 말씀하세요. (Please tell me anytime if you need help.)
지금 제 상황이 누군가를 도울 여유가 없어요. (My situation now doesn't have the room to help someone.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be in a difficult position / Embarrassing
vocabulary.example: 갑자기 어려운 질문을 받아서 정말 난감했어요. (I was in a really difficult position because I received a sudden difficult question.)
정중하게 거절하는 것도 대화의 기술이에요. (Declining politely is also a skill of conversation.)

vocabulary.meaning: To really want to do something
vocabulary.example: 도와주고 싶은 마음은 굴뚝같지만 시간이 없네요. (I really want to help, but I don't have time.)
미안해요, 오늘 저녁엔 선약이 있어서 못 가요. (I'm sorry, I can't go because I have a previous engagement this evening.)
